hypothesis: If a large language model is continually pre-trained on MOF-related articles and PubChem chemical data and subsequently fine-tuned on MOF-specific property datasets using MOFid-to-property mappings, then it will achieve lower MAE in predicting MOF band gaps and gas adsorption properties on held-out test sets than baseline models (CGCNN, SOAP, MOFormer), including under limited SFT data (1K/3K/10K), because the pre-training embeds MOF-relevant chemical knowledge into the model's text-based representations that 3D-structure–based baselines cannot leverage.
